feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Adults should have no more than one drink a day, a team of scientists says
entry.summary: Americans should limit their alcohol consumption to no more than one drink per day, according to a study published Tuesday in the Journal of Studies on Alcohol and Drugs

entry.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda
entry.summary: The Bundibugyo virus disease (BVD) outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo continues to evolve rapidly, with increasing case numbers, geographic spread, and cross-border transmission to Uganda. As of 6 June, a total of 515 confirmed cases, with 91 de…

entry.title: Scientists Found 2 Existing Drugs Could Reverse Alzheimer's Brain Damage in Mice
entry.summary: Scientists looking to combat Alzheimer's disease may have found two promising candidates.

entry.title: Targeting Cancer-Specific Mutations with RNA-Triggered Chromatin Shredding
entry.summary: Nature - Targeting Cancer-Specific Mutations with RNA-Triggered Chromatin Shredding

entry.title: GPR15-guided CD8+ T regulatory cells control intestinal inflammation
entry.summary: Nature - GPR15-guided CD8+ T regulatory cells control intestinal inflammation

entry.title: Cholesterol superfood that unclogs arteries is 'better than aspirin' doctor says
entry.summary: Dr Eric Berg DC, a health educator with 13.5 million YouTube subscribers, says one superfood is the 'number one' superfood for naturally clearing arteries and supporting heart health

entry.title: The late-night eating habit that may throw your gut health into chaos
entry.summary: If stress causes your digestive woes, eating late at night isn't doing you any favors.

entry.title: Greek Yogurt Vs. Regular Yogurt: Dietitians Reveal Which Is Healthier
entry.summary: Dietitians break down Greek yogurt vs. regular yogurt, including which delivers more protein, which is best for bone health, and what else you should know.

entry.title: Your earlobes may hold a sneaky clue about future heart disease
entry.summary: While there are some clear signs of heart disease, like shortness of breath or chest pain, others aren't as well-known, even when they're in plain sight.

entry.title: Column | Love coffee? Have the last cup at this time for a better night’s sleep.
entry.summary: Sorry coffee lovers, that late-day latte may be causing you to lose more sleep than you think.

entry.title: Vaccines have a savior you didn't see coming
entry.summary: Perspective: Health insurers are backing routine vaccines as MAHA reopens the schedule fight, and the logic of profit wins out.

entry.title: Childhood egg allergies fall as early introduction becomes more common, new study finds
entry.summary: Parents used to be advised to keep allergenic foods like eggs away from babies, especially if allergies ran in the family. But based on recent and evolving evidence, the advice is now almost the opposite – and new research suggests the shift in guidance is pa…

entry.title: Doctors Changed One Thing and Weight Gain Stopped
entry.summary: An endocrinologist’s new weight-care system halted population-level weight gain and could reshape obesity treatment across America.

entry.title: This Sweet Treat-Themed Game Is 100% Cozy And 200% Stress Free
entry.summary: Drag, drop, and merge sugary treats from a single wrapped candy all the way up to the legendary birthday cake.

entry.title: Weight-loss jab Wegovy to be offered on NHS in Scotland
entry.summary: The drug works as an appetite suppressant by mimicking a hormone that makes people feel fuller, which can help them lose weight.

entry.title: Research Connects an Underrated Nutrient with Healthier Aging
entry.summary: Learn what research suggests regarding zinc intake, exercise and biological aging. Plus, which foods provide zinc and what it does for your body.

entry.title: Vaccines for Bees, Shrimp Could Mark a 'Paradigm Shift'
entry.summary: Experimental vaccines aim to curb diseases costly to agriculture, aquaculture

entry.title: Tolkkinen: I grew up in the group responsible for low vaccination rates in rural Minnesota.
entry.summary: We were all vaxxed in the 1970s and 1980s. What changed and what should we do about it?

entry.title: The late-night eating habit that may throw your gut health into chaos
entry.summary: Eating late at night may compound the harmful impacts of stress on your digestive health and gut microbiome, according to recent research.

entry.title: Beyond ‘dad bod’: What an expanding middle may mean for metabolic health
entry.summary: Columnist Carrie Dennett shares how men can start making more meaningful changes to reduce excess visceral fat (fat stored deep within the abdominal cavity).

entry.title: What ProPublica Found in the Genetic Code of America’s Measles Outbreaks
entry.summary: The U.S. eliminated measles a quarter century ago, but to keep its “measles-free” designation, officials will have to make a strong case that measles is not continuously spreading within its borders. Our analysis shows how difficult that could be.

entry.title: AIIMS-trained gastroenterologist shares 6 reasons for the rise in colon cancer cases in young women in their 30s | Health
entry.summary: Colon cancer cases are on the rise, especially among the youth. Dr Sethi shares 6 reasons why more young women are getting diagnosed with the disease. | Health

entry.title: HIV enters the brain and doesn’t leave – paradoxically, drugs intended to reduce brain inflammation increase virus levels
entry.summary: Immune cells can carry HIV into the brain, leading to problems with memory and cognition. Blocking other immune cells from the brain can make matters worse.

entry.title: Oral Cancer Risk Could Be Raised by This Common Food
entry.summary: Experts suggest processed meat can elevate your risk of oral cancer. Learn why here, plus other ways you can reduce your risk.

entry.title: Why are so many young people getting cancer? What researchers do and don't know
entry.summary: Candidates for the trend are emerging, but are likely to vary from one type of tumor to another.

entry.title: '5 a day': Which fruit and veg are best for heart health and why?
entry.summary: Fewer than one in five people consume enough flavanols each day to help reduce their cardiovascular disease risk, even if they are following the '5 a day' recommendation for fruits and vegetable consumption, a study has found.

entry.title: Dementia is common for older adults living alone but many are not diagnosed, UCSF study finds
entry.summary: UCSF scientists who interviewed more than 100 older adults found that roughly a third showed signs of mild cognitive impairment and were not diagnosed.

entry.title: Postpartum Depression is Coming for Fathers
entry.summary: Postpartum depression is coming for fathers. Men are more involved parents than ever, and it’s leading to a crisis no one warned them about.

entry.title: As vaccination rates plunge in Pennsylvania schools, measles cases surge in largest outbreak in three decades
entry.summary: False

entry.title: Our Neighbors Asked to Speak With Us. Then They Showed Us Something We Can’t Ignore.
entry.summary: I don't think her solution is going to work out.

entry.title: Naked mole-rats age so slowly, resist cancer so well and survive oxygen loss so strangely that researchers now study them as one of nature’s best clues to human ageing.
entry.summary: The naked mole-rat is a small, hairless, more or less blind rodent that lives in colonies under the deserts of East Africa. It is about the size of a mouse, and a mouse lives around four years. The naked mole-rat lives more than thirty, with the oldest on rec…

entry.title: Why the U.S. Is Unprepared for a Potential Public Health Outbreak at the World Cup
entry.summary: The U.S. spent $625 million on World Cup security—none of it went to public health. Just days from the tournament, big concerns remain.

entry.title: Some fruits and vegetables are especially good for heart health
entry.summary: Including blueberries, plums, blackberries, broad beans or cherries (washed down with green tea) in your recommended five-a-day (five 80g portions of fruit and vegetables, recommended by the UK's NHS) may be the best way to a healthier heart, new research sug…

entry.title: 15 health myths medical experts wish would finally go away
entry.summary: 15 Health Myths Medical Experts Wish Would Finally Go Away Health advice has a funny way of sticking around long after science has moved on. Some myths get p...

entry.title: Dementia risk linked to nitrate in drinking water, study finds
entry.summary: A major long-term study of more than 54,000 adults found that where nitrate comes from may matter far more than how much you consume. People who got more nitrate from vegetables—roughly the amount in a cup of baby spinach a day—had a lower risk of developing …

entry.title: Children who sleep poorly ‘turn into depressed teenagers’
entry.summary: Academics support consistent bedtimes, reduced screen time before bed, physical activity during the day and a calm sleep environment

entry.title: In a first, human receives multiple pig organs
entry.summary: A patient received two kidneys and a whole liver from a pig, the first example of multi-organ xenotransplantation.

entry.title: Everyone Warned Me About The Side Effects Of GLP-1s. Nobody Warned Me About The Judgment.
entry.summary: "I didn’t tell anyone I was starting a GLP-1 for this exact reason."

entry.title: Ebola outbreak live updates: WHO and Africa CDC unveil $518 million plan as DRC cases pass 450
entry.summary: A Kenyan court has blocked a proposed U.S. Ebola quarantine facility that has triggered protests in the country.

entry.title: The Probiotic Breakthrough for Natural Anxiety Relief and Better Mental Health
entry.summary: A surprising gut-brain discovery suggests that anxiety could one day be treated with specially designed probiotics. Could anxiety be shaped, at least in part, by tiny organisms living in the gut? Research from Duke-NUS Medical School and the National Neurosci…

entry.title: World Cup creates perfect conditions for infectious diseases to spread – here are the biggest threats health experts are watching for
entry.summary: Respiratory diseases like measles and flu pose a much bigger threat to public health for the World Cup than does Ebola.

entry.title: Cancer Death of Retired SF Firefighter Reignites Calls to Reform Blue Shield Coverage
entry.summary: The death of San Francisco firefighter Ken Jones has renewed calls to reform Blue Shield, the city's designated insurance provider. The late firefighter’s battle to treat his stage 4 lung cancer first gained attention in January, with advocates now blaming de…

entry.title: Popular weight loss drug may slow down biological aging: Study
entry.summary: The study found that the pace of biological aging for people who are living with HIV slowed by nine percent.

entry.title: Huge Psilocybin Dose Has Incredible Effect on Elderly Dementia Patient
entry.summary: A radical new treatment may be emerging.

entry.title: You Can Tell How Miserable Someone Is By 10 Things They Care About Way Too Much
entry.summary: Even if they don't verbalize it, you can tell how miserable someone is by 10 things they care about way too much. They're often stuck in their heads, and it becomes obvious to those around them.

entry.title: Your earlobes may hold a sneaky clue about future heart disease
entry.summary: While there are some clear signs of heart disease, like shortness of breath or chest pain, others aren’t as well-known, even when they’re in plain sight.

entry.title: 4 Gentle Exercises That Build Shoulder Strength Better Than Heavy Lifting After 60
entry.summary: A trainer shares 4 gentle shoulder exercises after 60 that rebuild strength with light resistance, no heavy lifting.

entry.title: This Simple Bedtime Tweak Cuts Your Risk of Major Cardiac Events in Half
entry.summary: Getting a solid eight hours of sleep per night sounds wonderful in theory. In reality? Between late-night TV, racing thoughts at 11 p.m. and the occasional g...

entry.title: Most people ignore Fatty Liver until it's too late: Liver transplant surgeon explains why weight loss could change everything
entry.summary: For years, fatty liver disease was often brushed aside as a minor finding on an ultrasound report. Many people never felt any symptoms. Others assumed it was a condition linked only to alcohol consumption. Today, doctors are seeing a very different picture.Ac…

entry.title: Woman Thought She Had a Stomach Bug, and Doctors Thought She Had an Autoimmune Disease. It Was Actually Something More Deadly
entry.summary: Audrey Leishman was placed in a medical coma due to her health issues
